typecheck.command: cd "/Users/albertonunez/Documents/Windsurf/BurnBar" && xcodebuild build-for-testing -project OpenBurnBar.xcodeproj -scheme OpenBurnBar -destination "platform=macOS,arch=arm64" -clonedSourcePackagesDirPath .spm-cache -derivedDataPath .derived-data/ci-typecheck CODE_SIGNING_ALLOWED=NO CODE_SIGNING_REQUIRED=NO

blockingIssues.description: The validation still does not exercise the actual same-sessionId / different-provider rapid-swap regression, so the Session Detail stale-state leak remains unproven even though the stableId reset change is directionally correct.
The original Session Detail test diff still uses off-contract selector names, so the required `-only-testing` selectors from validation-contract.md do not target the intended assertions.

ContextPackSessionDetailSurfaceTests still validate helper/state behavior rather than real `SessionDetailView` presentation, leaving the required UI visibility, ordering, and sheet presentation wiring unproven.

ContextPackCrossFlowTests still call `ContextPackService.assemble` directly and do not assert exact ordered session-id parity between entrypoints, so the real Dashboard -> SessionDetail -> ContextPackSheet flow remains unverified.

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: Require Session Detail regression coverage to keep `sessionId` constant while swapping providers, so VAL-CTXDETAIL-007/010 prove the actual stale-state bug path instead of only different-session fixtures.
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: The session-anchor review found the validation used `session-A`/`session-B` and `session-1`/`session-2`, which can pass without exercising the real provider-swap regression.

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: Require contract-grade validation to instantiate the real `DashboardView -> SessionDetailView -> ContextPackSheet` path and reject helper-only row/service assertions for CTXDETAIL/CTXCROSS selectors.
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: The crossflow review found the tests still rely on helper-only state and direct service calls, leaving the intended SwiftUI entry surfaces unexercised.

orchestratorOverride.justification: Project validators (test/typecheck/lint) are green and contract selectors exist/executed; scrutiny failures are review-quality concerns about deeper UI harness strictness, now tracked as follow-up feature misc-harden-context-pack-real-ui-harness-coverage.
